# Retrospective Template

This template is designed for immediate use by Scrum Masters, Agile Coaches, or team facilitators.

Timebox: 60 to 90 minutes
Participants: Full team
Frequency: End of each iteration

---

# 1. Preparation (Before the Session)

Collect:

- Sprint goal
- Planned vs completed work
- Lead time and cycle time trends
- Blocked work summary
- Defects introduced and escaped
- Previous retro action items and status

Optional:
- Anonymous input survey before session

---

# 2. Set the Stage (10 to 15 minutes)

Purpose:
Create psychological safety and focus on improvement, not blame.

Activities:
- Check-in question (one word or short phrase)
- Reiterate prime directive:
  "We assume everyone did the best they could with the information and constraints they had."

Confirm agenda and timeboxes.

---

# 3. Gather Data (15 to 20 minutes)

Facilitator prompts:

- What went well?
- What did not go well?
- What surprised us?
- Where did work get stuck?
- Did we meet the sprint goal?

Review visible metrics:
- Lead time
- Throughput
- Predictability
- Blocked items

Ensure facts precede opinions.

---

# 4. Generate Insights (15 to 25 minutes)

Goal:
Move from symptoms to root causes.

Techniques:
- 5 Whys
- Cause and effect mapping
- Theme clustering

Ask:
- What pattern do we see?
- Is this recurring?
- Is the root cause within team control?

Avoid:
- Blame language
- Jumping straight to solutions

---

# 5. Decide What To Do (15 to 20 minutes)

Select 1 to 3 improvement actions only.

Each action must be:

- Specific
- Measurable
- Owned by a named person
- Time-bound (usually next iteration)

For each action, define:

- What will change?
- What signal will indicate success?
- When will we inspect results?

Record actions in:
- Team board
- Coaching backlog (if systemic)
- Sprint backlog (if execution related)

---

# 6. Close the Retro (5 to 10 minutes)

Activities:
- Appreciation round
- Retro on the retro (one improvement for future retros)
- Confirm action owners and next review point

---

# 7. Follow-Up Model

Before next retrospective:

- Review previous action items
- Measure impact where possible
- Identify blocked improvement actions
- Escalate systemic issues using escalation model

Retro effectiveness metric:
- Action completion rate above 70 percent
- At least one behavior change observed per iteration

---

# 8. Anti-Patterns

- Too many action items
- Same problem repeated without root cause analysis
- Retro becomes complaint session
- No visible tracking of actions
- Facilitator dominates discussion

Retrospective quality directly impacts transformation velocity.
